title: toxicodendron orientale subsp. orientale
intro: Toxicodendron orientale subsp. orientale, also known as the Chinese poison ivy, is a plant species belonging to the Anacardiaceae family, characterized by its glossy leaves and urushiol oil, which causes severe skin irritation and allergic reactions in most people. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. The health benefits of using Toxicodendron orientale subsp. orientale include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be obtained from reputable suppliers or prepared under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
